response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps, plus genuine systems experience in DLang and some Java/C++ exposure at National Instruments on an IDE product. However, the role demands strong, current expertise in Java, Kotlin, JVM internals, and the Spring ecosystem, none of which appear meaningfully on the resume. Recent work is TypeScript/Node and AI tooling, with no demonstrated Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate or Kotlin background, making this a weak fit for a Senior IDE/JVM tooling role.
strengths: Prior IDE/tooling experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI in C/C++)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing libraries
Broad systems background including low-latency services and developer tooling
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only listed Java as a skill with no recent depth
No Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience shown
Recent work is TS/Node-focused, lacking deep JVM internals exposure

key_factors.factor: Prior IDE tooling work at National Instruments
